issues.description: UserTableProps has 17 props covering filtering, selection, pagination, and export concerns. A consumer needing only rendering has to accept handler props for features it may not use (e.g., onExportCsv, onSelectUsersChange). Consider splitting into focused sub-interfaces or delegating filter/selection state to a container hook so the presentational table only receives data + minimal callbacks.
issues.recommendation: Extract filter/selection state to a useUserTableState hook and pass a smaller, focused props set to the table component. Alternatively, split into UserTableFilters, UserTableBody, and UserTablePagination sub-components each with narrow interfaces.

issues.description: The BlockEditor switch on block.type (5 cases: text, image, video, link, url) is an OCP concern — adding a new block type requires modifying this file. The current count (5) is below the >8 critical threshold, but the pattern is established enough that a block-type registry (e.g., a blockEditors map keyed by type) would make extensions additive rather than modificatory. The related DisplayBlockRenderer has the same pattern.
issues.recommendation: Consider a registry map: const BLOCK_EDITORS: Record<BlockType, ComponentType<...>> = { text: TextBlockEditor, image: ImageBlockEditor, ... }. New types then only require adding an entry, not editing the switch. Only worth doing if the block type list is expected to grow.

refactoredFilesVerification.notes: Mutations fully removed. Uses onToggle(field, value) and onDelete() callbacks. No direct mutation hooks. SRP is clean — pure presentation with delegated actions.
Correctly holds the mutation hooks (useReorderProducts, useToggleProduct, useDeleteProduct) and passes callbacks down to ProductTableRow. Container/presenter split is correct.
All data-fetching and mutation logic delegated to usePaymentMethodsManager. Component is now a thin presenter consuming the hook's return values.

New hook correctly encapsulates all data-fetching, mutations, and UI state (expandedId). SRP is appropriate — single responsibility of managing the payment methods page state. Uses useTranslations for one confirm dialog string; acceptable.

patterns.name: Switch-based renderer pattern
patterns.description: Multiple components (DisplayBlockRenderer, BlockEditor, SectionRenderer, SectionCard, ReceivedStatusBadge, StatusRow) use switch statements for type-to-component or type-to-config mapping. All are 2–5 cases — below the >8 OCP threshold. The pattern is intentional and consistent across the codebase. Only flagging BlockEditor as a suggestion given it has 5 cases and is likely to grow.
patterns.verdict: Acceptable — not a SOLID violation at current scale.

patterns.name: Application hooks with Supabase access
patterns.description: useSellerProfiles, useProducts, and useDelegateSearch use Supabase directly in application/hooks via useSupabase()/createBrowserSupabaseClient. This is within the expected architecture — application hooks ARE the infrastructure adapter in this project's layer model. No presentation-layer direct API calls were found.
patterns.verdict: Compliant — DIP is respected through hook abstraction.
